pattern: Backtracking fills k buckets to a fixed target sum, pruning by sorting descending, skipping over-target placements, and abandoning a bucket that fails to start with the largest remaining item.
hints: The target per subset is the total sum divided by k; if that is not an integer or any element exceeds it, it is impossible.
Try to fill one bucket at a time up to the target, recursing to the next bucket when one is complete.
Sort descending and place items into the current bucket; prune by skipping items that overflow the target, and if the first item of a fresh bucket cannot lead to a solution, fail fast.

edge_inputs.description: k equals length needs every element equal
one large element exceeds the target
divisible sum but no valid grouping

stress.description: construct k buckets of random items then shuffle, guaranteeing a valid partition exists
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randrange(2, 5)
    n_per = max(1, 16 // k)
    target = rng.randrange(3, 12)
    nums = []
    for _ in range(k):
        remaining = target
        cnt = rng.randrange(1, n_per + 1)
        for j in range(cnt - 1):
            slots_left = cnt - 1 - j
            if remaining <= slots_left:
                break
            x = rng.randrange(1, remaining - slots_left + 1)
            nums.append(x)
            remaining -= x
        nums.append(remaining)
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: every generated array partitions into k equal-sum subsets by construction
